note: Section numbers per Selkirk 2026 summary (https://www.selkirk.com/blogs/pickleball-education/2026-usa-pickleball-rulebook-changes-key-updates-for-the-new-year) and The Dink's 7-rule brief. **Section numbers should be verified against the primary USAP PDF before user-facing citation.** 2026 rulebook uses 4-digit numbering (e.g., 2070, 2221) per secondary sources.

changes.summary: A ball that legally crosses the net and bounces in the receiving court, then contacts the net post, is not an automatic fault.
changes.impact: reduces marginal faults; favors the hitter

changes.summary: Line calls must be made promptly. Delayed 'out' calls after partner conferences are disallowed.
Officials may issue warnings and penalties during pre-match briefings and warmup, not only during play.
Explicit ejection authority for assault causing injury, and for damage to venue property.
Rally scoring formats are formally documented in the rulebook. Remains optional for many events; restricted in some championship-qualifying events.
changes.impact: provides sanctioned reference for rally scoring; does not mandate it

changes.summary: Eligibility criteria, assistive-device rules, two-bounce allowance, and hybrid mixed-ability play introduced.
The word 'clearly' is added to all three volley-serve requirements (below waist, paddle head below wrist, upward arc). Borderline serves are now faulted.
changes.impact: tightens serve legality; favors referee discretion against borderline serves

changes.summary: A visible second ball (e.g., peeking from a pocket) during a live rally is now an explicit fault.
Paddles must display 'USA Pickleball Approved' marking and appear on the approved list. Pre-match inspection is mandatory.
changes.impact: raises enforcement of paddle certification; notable for pros using prototypes

changes.summary: Legal double-hit rule extended to triple-plus hits within one continuous motion.
changes.impact: expands what's legal in unusual contacts

caveats: Section numbers marked 'tbd' or citing the older 4.A.5-family scheme must be verified against the primary USAP PDF before publishing as user-facing rule citations. Current entries rely on Selkirk and The Dink as secondary sources.
The change document (USAP-Rulebook-Change-Document.pdf) is the authoritative delta reference and should be the canonical source for user-facing 'what changed in 2026' widgets.

Phase 1 ingestion: pull the rulebook PDF once per year (post-December publication), extract changes, and render in a 'Rule Changes' module. Do not attempt to render the full rulebook in-app — link out.
